NOTICE OF REMOTE APPEARANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 76047869) FILED BY PETITIONER BLAY, LARRY
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-16-25 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-16-2025 HEARING RE: PETITIONER LARRY BLAY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT EVEREST DENALI INSURANCE COMPANY TO PARTICIPATE IN BINDING ARBITRATION AND FOR SANCTIONS OF $2,500 AGAINST KAROLINE KITLOWSKI; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF LARRY BLAY'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION PURSUANT TO INSURANCE CODE 11580.2(F) AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART, AND CONTINUED FOR FURTHER HEARING TO APRIL 30, 2025 (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Motion To Compel Defendant Everest Denali Insurance Company To Participate In Binding Arbitration And For Sanctions Of $2,500 Against Karoline Kitlowski; Declaration Of David L. Hart
Off Calendar Apr-25-2025 Off Calendar

Motion To Compel Defendant Everest Denali Insurance Company To Participate In Binding Arbitration And For Sanctions Of $2,500 Against Karoline Kitlowski; Declaration Of David L. Hart
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Continued To Apr-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONER LARRY BLAY's Motion To Compel Defendant Everest Denali Insurance Company To Participate In Binding Arbitration And For Sanctions Of $2,500 Against Karoline Kitlowski.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Larry Blay's motion to compel arbitration pursuant to Insurance Code 11580.2(f) and for sanctions is granted in part, denied in part, and continued for further hearing to April 30, 2025.

Blay moves to compel arbitration. Defendant Everest Denali Insurance Company does not oppose arbitration but contends that it is premature. The parties have been unable to agree to an arbitrator.

The court grants the motion and compels arbitration. Pursuant to CCP 1281.6, the court nominates the following five individuals as potential arbitrators: Hon. David Abbott (ret.) (Judicate West); Thomas H.R. Denver (ADR Services); Hon. Richard S. Flier (ret.) (ADR Services); Hon. Maria-Elena James (ret.) (ADR Services); David J. Stock (ADR Services).

The court continues the matter for hearing for selection of the arbitrator to April 30, 2025. If the parties have agreed to an arbitrator from the above list before then, they are to submit a joint filing notifying the court and no appearance will be necessary. If the parties are unable to agree, the court will select an arbitrator from the above list.

Defendant contends that arbitration is premature because Plaintiff has recently begun new courses of treatment. Any contentions about the timing of arbitration will be resolved by the arbitrator if the parties are unable to agree.

Plaintiff seeks sanctions. The court denies the request.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)z
